HOUSTON'S TRUSTED ROOFERS SINCE 1958

68 years of storm-damage expertise on your side.

Free for Houston property owners.

We've navigated Alicia, Ike, Harvey, and Beryl. Before you call your insurance company, let Meza secure the objective initial evaluation your property deserves.

Most homeowners jeopardize their storm claims by calling their insurer before documenting the damage hidden to the untrained eye. That lack of precise physical proof leaves your property entirely unprotected.

We reverse that risk. Our initial evaluation is 100% free. We secure the high-resolution photographic evidence and objective structural proof needed to establish the undeniable truth of your roof's condition.

The Meza Difference

Stop guessing from the driveway—put Terry’s technical expertise on your roof.

The Reality of Storm Claims

Why Objective Structural Proof Matters Before You Call Your Insurer

Without Meza — When You File Alone
Blind Contact

You call your insurer first, leaving your claim vulnerable to an immediate denial track.

The "Wear & Tear" Trap

Without historical data, insurers routinely classify storm damage as pre-existing structural decay.

Uncontested Adjustments

An adjuster walks your roof alone — minimizing findings with no opposing documentation on record.

The Lowball Estimate

You receive a payout that doesn't cover Texas materials and labor — with no data to challenge it.

With Meza — When You Partner with Us
Prepared Position

You approach backed by precise physical data establishing the true condition of your property.

Historical Fact

We cross-reference your assessment date with NOAA storm data to document the storm-date timeline.

Initial Evaluation Documentation

Our team captures high-resolution photographic proof of structural damage for your replacement project.

True Market Value

We provide a transparent, line-item contractor estimate reflecting honest local replacement costs.

Already Denied or Disputed?

Your Claim May Not Be Over.

We operate strictly as construction experts — but we connect our replacement clients with independent consumer protection attorneys who can review your denied or disputed claim.

Talk to Us → (346) 281-4358

The Process

Step by Step: How Meza General Contractors Documents Your Storm Damage

From precision documentation to final build — we manage the entire structural process.

1
Same / Next Day

Initial Evaluation

Our team performs a comprehensive initial evaluation to document what insurance adjusters often miss.

2
Storm Verification

Meteorological Verification

We align our physical damage findings with official NOAA historical storm data for your neighborhood, creating an objective timeline that helps address “wear and tear” or “neglect” concerns.

3
Critical Step

Documentation Review

We review the damage documentation with the homeowner so they understand what information is available before contacting the insurer.

4
On-Site

On-Site Technical Support

We meet your adjuster on-site to ensure all documented physical damage is accurately reviewed.

5
Execution

Approval

We mobilize. Subject to your specific policy terms, your primary cost is typically limited to your Homeowner’s Responsibility Share. If there is a policy gap or variance, we offer flexible financing options for qualifying homeowners.

6
If Denied

Denial — Legal

If your valid claim is denied, we can connect you with independent attorney resources for disputed or denied claims. Meza General Contractors does not provide legal advice.

Important Notice: Meza General Contractors is a roofing and general contracting company. We are not a public adjuster, insurance company, or law firm. We do not determine insurance coverage, negotiate claims, or guarantee claim approval. Our role is to inspect visible property damage, document our findings, provide contractor estimates, and complete roofing work when authorized by the homeowner.

Houston's roofing specialists since 1958. We provide residential and commercial roofing, storm damage documentation, and complete roof replacement across a 100-mile service radius.
